_sources.infra_power: Enterprise 802.11ac AP measured idle ~8 W, active ~9.6 W TX / 8.8 W RX (Bolla et al. 2011; Cisco AP measurements). Configured as: idle_w=8.0, tx_w=1.6 (increment), rx_w=0.8 (increment). Q1 energy from ns-3 WifiRadioEnergyModel with idle-baseline subtraction. The 8 W idle is shared across all AP clients and not charged to a single flow. NOTE: infra energy normalized by emergent airtime (single STA), NOT by cell capacity — may charge more per bit than capacity-amortized model.
_sources.device_power: Halperin et al., 'Demystifying 802.11n Power Consumption', HotPower 2010 (Intel 5300: idle 0.84 W, RX 0.94 W, TX 1.28 W). Values scaled for 802.11ac. Configured as: idle_w=0.85, tx_w=0.55 (increment), rx_w=0.15 (increment). Q1 energy from ns-3 WifiRadioEnergyModel with idle-baseline subtraction (SLEEP not yet active).
_sources.psm: 802.11 PSM / U-APSD introduced in 802.11 (1997) and 802.11e (2005). Currently analytical placeholder (Fase 1); will be implemented as ns-3 SLEEP state in Fase 3. doze_w ~25 mW per Halperin et al. 2010 and Pering et al. 2006 'Coolspots'. tail_s = 0.1 s (100 ms inactivity timeout, conservative midpoint of 50-500 ms range). burst_gap_s = 0.05 s: minimum gap to consider two bursts separate.
